req.title: Update patient (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers PUT /Patient/{id} with clinician role. We need: (1) a positive EP case confirming successful update and history preservation, (2) a negative NEG/DT case for version conflict returning 409, (3) a negative NEG case for missing mandatory element, (4) a negative NEG case for permission denial (non-clinician), (5) a boundary BVA case for the versionId value at the exact current version vs. version+1. State transitions (STC) from 'active' patient record through versioned update are also covered. Decision Table heuristic maps the combination of correct role + correct version + all required fields to 200/201, and deviations to error codes.

tests.name: test_update_patient_valid_clinician_preserves_history
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
A patient resource exists with known id and versionId via <existing_patient> fixture
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Perform PUT /Patient/{id} with valid demographics payload and correct If-Match versionId
Perform GET /Patient/{id}/_history to retrieve version history
expected: Response body contains updated family name
History endpoint returns at least 2 entries
Previous version is present in history bundle
trace: R-HLT-053: new version stored; history preserved
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_valid_clinician_preserves_history(base_url, clinician_auth_token, existing_patient):
    patient_id = existing_patient["id"]
    version_id = existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{version_id}"'
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": version_id},
        "name": [{"family": "UpdatedFamily", "given": ["TestGiven"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1990-01-01"
    }
    put_resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    body = put_resp.json()
    assert body["name"][0]["family"] == "UpdatedFamily"
    new_version = int(body["meta"]["versionId"])
    assert new_version == int(version_id) + 1
    hist_resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2
    versions = [e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"] for e in entries]
    assert str(version_id) in versions

tests.name: test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
A patient resource exists with known id and versionId via <existing_patient> fixture
A stale versionId (current - 1) is constructed to simulate conflict
actions: Perform PUT /Patient/{id} with an outdated If-Match versionId header
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with conflict details
trace: R-HLT-053: 409 on version conflict error pathway
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409(base_url, clinician_auth_token, existing_patient):
    patient_id = existing_patient["id"]
    current_version = int(existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"])
    stale_version = str(current_version - 1) if current_version > 1 else "0"
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{stale_version}"'
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": stale_version},
        "name": [{"family": "ConflictFamily", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1990-01-01"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_update_patient_missing_mandatory_birthdate_returns_400
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
A patient resource exists with known id and versionId via <existing_patient> fixture
birthDate is a mandatory field per fhir-lite profile
actions: Perform PUT /Patient/{id} omitting the mandatory birthDate field
expected: Response body is an OperationOutcome indicating missing required element
trace: R-HLT-053: missing mandatory elements negative path
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_missing_mandatory_birthdate_returns_400(base_url, clinician_auth_token, existing_patient):
    patient_id = existing_patient["id"]
    version_id = existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{version_id}"'
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": version_id},
        "name": [{"family": "NoDateFamily", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "gender": "female"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = [i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert any("birthDate" in d or "required" in d.lower() for d in issues)

tests.name: test_update_patient_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: A non-clinician (e.g. read-only auditor) token available via <auditor_auth_token> fixture
A patient resource exists with known id and versionId via <existing_patient> fixture
actions: Perform PUT /Patient/{id} using auditor (non-clinician) token with otherwise valid payload
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome indicating insufficient permissions
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_non_clinician_role_returns_403(base_url, auditor_auth_token, existing_patient):
    patient_id = existing_patient["id"]
    version_id = existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auditor_auth_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{version_id}"'
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": version_id},
        "name": [{"family": "ForbiddenFamily", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1985-06-15"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_update_patient_exact_current_versionid_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
A patient resource exists with known id and versionId via <existing_patient> fixture
The exact current versionId string (neither stale nor future) is used in If-Match
actions: Perform PUT /Patient/{id} with If-Match set to the exact current versionId (boundary: current == accepted)
Immediately perform a second PUT with the same original versionId to confirm it is now stale
expected: First PUT response status is 200 and versionId increments by exactly 1
Second PUT response status is 409 confirming boundary at current+1
trace: R-HLT-053: version conflict error pathway BVA at exact current versionId boundary
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_exact_current_versionid_boundary_accepted(base_url, clinician_auth_token, existing_patient):
    patient_id = existing_patient["id"]
    version_id = existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{version_id}"'
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": version_id},
        "name": [{"family": "BoundaryFamily", "given": ["BVA"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1992-12-31"
    }
    first_resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert first_resp.status_code == 200
    new_version = int(first_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"])
    assert new_version == int(version_id) + 1
    second_resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert second_resp.status_code == 409
